Register as a Delegate

On January 24th, thousands of new token holders will be looking for the right community member to delegate their voting power to before claiming their locked tokens. Why not become a delegate and help guide the direction of the Obol Collective? This involves:

Appearing in the Delegate registry on claim day

Campaigning for votes

Increasing your influence in Obol Collective

Shaping the future of Obol

Helping projects qualify for the Obol Retroactive Fund.

Watch for phishing attempts

Be sure this domain matches the url shared by the @Obol_Collective team.

Timeline

  • Dec 2nd

    Call for Delegates

  • Jan 21st

    Airdrop checker

  • Jan 24th

    Claim & delegate locked airdrop

  • Feb 3rd

    Obol RAF round

  • TBD (Subject to governance)

    Unlock airdrop

FAQs

What does it mean to delegate tokens?

Only delegated tokens can participate in governance (e.g., Token House and Obol RAF). Delegating lets someone vote and participate on your behalf.

Why be a delegate?

Being a delegate allows you to actively participate in the decision-making process of the collective by representing the interests and opinions of token holders who have delegated their voting power to you. It gives you the opportunity to have a direct impact on governance.

Do I keep my tokens if I delegate?

Yes, you retain ownership of your tokens even if you delegate them. Delegation only transfers the voting power associated with the tokens, allowing the delegate to vote on your behalf. You can sell or transfer your tokens at any time.
